habitat: Cosmopolitan temperate weed of lawns, meadows, and roadsides.
traditionalUsesEn: Bitter digestive tonic; traditional diuretic use of leaves; roasted root beverage.
traditionalUsesDe: Bitterstoff für die Verdauung; traditionell harntreibende Blätter; geröstete Wurzel als Getränk.
activeCompounds: Sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, inulin (root), potassium
preparation: Leaf salad or tea; root decoction or roasted coffee substitute.
cautions: Asteraceae allergy; bile duct obstruction needs medical care, not self-treatment.
historicalNotes: Name from French dent-de-lion; globalized with European settlement.
